title: Nhandiroba scandens
intro: Nhandiroba scandens, also known as Annona coriacea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to the Brazilian Amazon, and traditional medicine has long recognized its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nhandiroba scandens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nhandiroba scandens is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource for local communities in the Amazon region.

It is native to the tropical regions of South America and is also found in other parts of the continent. The classification of Nhandiroba scandens is further broken down into the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants such as gentians and coffee. Nhandiroba scandens is a relatively understudied species, and more research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.
